driverGps.environment: Android API 35 emulator connected to the isolated non-production E2E stack
evidence: authenticated Driver location command accepted
Redis liveness refreshed
PostGIS location history persisted
authorized Admin Socket.IO listener received one event

mobileDeviceCapture.environment: Android API 35 emulator connected to the isolated non-production E2E stack
mobileDeviceCapture.privacyReview: Approved: simulated location only; no coordinates, credentials, tokens, email addresses, or personal notifications are visible
evidence: Driver entered Online and the authenticated location command was accepted
PostGIS retained the last location snapshot and the Android location foreground service was active only while Online
Driver returned Offline, the foreground service stopped, and the database online flag changed to false

productionAndroidGpsCapture.environment: Android API 35 x86_64 emulator using the deployed Railway API and Supabase PostgreSQL/PostGIS/Realtime stack
productionAndroidGpsCapture.build: Driver debug APK targeting Android SDK 36, built from sourceHead plus the uncommitted GPS accuracy and session-recovery fixes recorded by this capture
productionAndroidGpsCapture.privacyReview: Approved: synthetic temporary Driver only; no coordinates, email address, credential, token, provider key, or personal notification is visible; image metadata stripped and production test rows removed after verification
evidence: Explicit Online started an Android location foreground service after runtime permission approval
Screen-off simulated movement advanced the production PostGIS history while the foreground service remained active
Airplane-mode samples were buffered with their original timestamps and flushed after connectivity returned
Unexpected process termination followed by relaunch refreshed the session, restored Online through the API, and restarted foreground tracking
A paired authenticated production smoke received the GPS event through an authorized private Supabase Broadcast channel
The temporary Driver profile and all captured production GPS rows were deleted after the smoke

customerLoginCapture.environment: Android API 35 emulator; Customer app-launch screen before authentication routing
customerLoginCapture.privacyReview: Approved: no coordinates, credentials, tokens, email addresses, or personal notifications are visible; the WebP contains no EXIF/GPS profile

customerAuthCapture.environment: Android API 35 x86_64 emulator; Customer debug APK public authentication screens
customerAuthCapture.privacyReview: Approved: sign-in to registration and back without entered credentials, coordinates, tokens, email addresses, or personal notifications

mobileRoleCapture.environment: Android API 35 x86_64 emulator connected to the isolated foodflow-batch4-e2e stack
mobileRoleCapture.build: Flutter debug APKs built from the current working tree with customer and driver flavors
mobileRoleCapture.location: Fixed simulated GPS only; no Google Maps API key is used
mobileRoleCapture.privacyReview: Deterministic local seed identities only. Passwords remain masked; profile contact and vehicle values are synthetic seed data; no tokens, provider keys, production accounts, or real personal data are visible
evidence: Customer public sign-in and registration navigation was recorded without entering credentials
Driver authenticated through the local NestJS API; active-delivery Home explicitly showed that GPS sharing must resume while Offline
Driver earnings and profile surfaces rendered from deterministic seed data
Customer and Driver stills were visually reviewed after metadata stripping

gifs.source: Android screenrecord from the privacy-reviewed Customer debug APK public authentication screens
gifs.privacyReview: No entered credentials, coordinates, tokens, email addresses, or personal notifications
Uses only the four privacy-reviewed deterministic local Driver frames; metadata stripped

productionRoleSmokeCapture.environment: Canonical Vercel Admin and Restaurant production aliases backed by the Railway API and Supabase production database
productionRoleSmokeCapture.browser: User-selected Google Chrome through the Chrome browser channel
productionRoleSmokeCapture.fixture: Four exact temporary @example.invalid FoodFlow identities and one temporary restaurant; all rows were deactivated and deleted after capture
productionRoleSmokeCapture.privacyReview: Approved: only synthetic fixture labels and aggregate zero-state data are visible; no password, bearer token, provider key, real account, order, address, coordinate, or personal browser content is present
evidence: Admin authenticated to /vi/overview
Restaurant authenticated to its own /vi/orders queue
Both protected routes rejected the retained browser sessions after fixture cleanup
Final inventory returned zero users, profiles, restaurants, orders, and driver location rows

webCapture.environment: Isolated foodflow-batch4-e2e Docker stack with deterministic seed data
webCapture.privacyReview: Deterministic local seed data only; no production credentials, tokens, provider keys, or personal browser content are visible
